steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Calculate the area of circle M
steps.content:
Use the circle area formula with radius 3 mm:
$$A_M = \pi(3)^2 = 9\pi \text{ mm}^2$$

steps.title: Add the areas to find the total
steps.content:
Area of circle M is $9\pi$ mm² and area of circle N is $169\pi$ mm² (given). Adding them:
$$9\pi + 169\pi = 178\pi \text{ mm}^2$$
That confirms D is correct.
